name: confirm-add-duplicate-shows-noop-feedback
description: User at WEB_CONFIRM types 'Add facials' but Facials is already in the services list. Bot must inform the user 'Facials already in your services' (info icon, not a checkmark) and re-show the summary — NOT silently fall through to a 'what would you like to change?' hint. Earlier behavior: applyListOpLocal returned affected=[] and mutateWdForField returned null, leading to confused UX where the bot looked like it ignored the user. Now mutateWdForField emits a noop label and the caller renders ℹ️ instead of ✅.

reply_not_contains: What would you like to change
